criteria.label: Define self-supervised learning and distinguish it from supervised and unsupervised learning.
criteria.example: Write three sentences: where SSL labels come from, one supervised counterexample with human labels, and one unsupervised method without a pretext prediction target.

criteria.label: Describe a pretext task and identify its input, pseudo-label, and learned output.
criteria.example: For MLM on "The cat sat on the mat", show masked input, pseudo-label token, and state that contextual embeddings are the intended product.

criteria.label: Name pretext tasks for text and vision and match each to a foundation-model example.
criteria.example: Pair MLM with BERT, next-token with GPT, and image–text contrastive with CLIP in a short table.

criteria.label: Explain why pretrain-then-fine-tune is the standard SSL deployment pattern.
criteria.example: Argue that low pretext loss does not guarantee chat quality, so a labeled fine-tuning or alignment phase on the product task is still required.
